wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ClinicalLog CMS project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a Laravel-based Content Management System for medical education institutions, its dual role as both CMS and marketing website, and its core value proposition. Document the target audience, key features including landing page management, appointment scheduling, and user administration. Include the technology stack overview with Laravel 13.8+, Laravel Breeze authentication, Tailwind CSS, and Vite. Provide architectural context showing how this system integrates content management with user-facing interfaces. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common use cases for medical education institutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: MVC Architecture & Design Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the MVC architecture implementation in ClinicalLog CMS. Explain the Model-View-Controller pattern with Laravel conventions, detailing how controllers handle HTTP requests, models manage data access and validation, and views render content using Blade templating. Document the service provider registration system, dependency injection patterns, and component lifecycle management. Include examples of controller inheritance, model relationships, and view component composition. Explain how the application follows SOLID principles and design patterns like Repository and Factory patterns. Address request-response flow, middleware integration, and error handling throughout the MVC stack.

wiki_catalogs.name: Landing Page Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the landing page management system. Explain the centralized content editing interface that allows administrators to modify all landing page sections in a single location. Document the multi-section editing capabilities including hero section, about section, dashboard section, CTA section, and testimonial management. Detail the real-time preview functionality and section visibility controls. Include validation rules for each content field, image upload handling with size and format restrictions, and the JSON-based data structure used for complex content like navbar links, benefits, steps, testimonials, and pricing plans. Provide practical examples of content modification workflows and troubleshooting common editing issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the user management system in ClinicalLog CMS. Document the User model structure, including database schema, attributes, and relationships. Explain user registration processes, profile management capabilities, and user data validation. Cover administrative user management features including user creation, editing, deletion, and role assignment. Detail the profile update functionality, password management, and user preferences. Include examples of user data manipulation, bulk operations, and user search/filtering capabilities. Address user data security, privacy considerations, and audit trails.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for feature CRUD operations in ClinicalLog CMS. Document the complete create, read, update, and delete functionality implemented in the FeatureController. Explain the store() method for creating new features with validation, position assignment, and icon handling. Detail the update() method for modifying existing features including icon replacement, position changes, and data validation. Cover the destroy() method for feature deletion with proper cleanup and position adjustment. Include request validation rules, error handling, and success messaging. Provide code examples showing typical usage patterns and integration with the admin interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contact Form Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the contact form processing component of the appointment management system. Document the form validation rules including name, email, WhatsApp number, institution, demo date, demo time, and notes fields. Explain the request validation process, data sanitization, and error handling mechanisms. Detail the form submission workflow from frontend to backend processing, including AJAX handling and JSON response generation. Include examples of customizing validation rules, adding new form fields, and implementing CAPTCHA verification. Address form security considerations, rate limiting, and spam prevention measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for content management endpoints in ClinicalLog CMS. Document the landing page management API with GET/PUT methods for retrieving and updating landing page configurations including hero sections, features, testimonials, and dashboard settings. Detail the feature management API covering CRUD operations for features with sorting, positioning, and icon management. Include request/response schemas, validation rules, authentication requirements, and error handling patterns. Provide practical examples of content updates, bulk operations, and real-time preview functionality. Address content validation, image upload integration, and section visibility controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration Files & Schema Evolution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ClinicalLog CMS migration files and database schema evolution. Detail each migration file's purpose, table structure, field definitions, and relationships. Explain the chronological evolution of the database schema from initial setup to advanced features like landing pages, features, testimonials, and appointments. Document indexing strategies, foreign key constraints, and data type choices. Include migration rollback procedures, schema modification patterns, and best practices for maintaining database consistency during development. Provide examples of adding new tables, modifying existing schemas, and handling backward compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Blade Templating System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Blade templating system in ClinicalLog CMS. Detail the layout inheritance pattern with app.blade.php as the master template, admin.blade.php for administrative sections, and guest.blade.php for public-facing pages. Explain the component-based approach using Blade components like application-logo, auth-session-status, input-error, and form elements. Document the section-based content management system with @yield directives, @stack functionality for dynamic content injection, and conditional rendering based on landing page configurations. Include practical examples of layout composition, component usage patterns, and data binding between controllers and views. Address the integration with Laravel's view factory system and caching mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for unit testing implementation in ClinicalLog CMS. Explain PHPUnit unit testing framework setup, test case structure, and individual component testing approaches. Document testing patterns for models, validation logic, and business rules. Include examples of testing User model methods, Feature model operations, and custom validation rules. Cover test isolation techniques, mocking dependencies, and assertion strategies. Address unit test best practices, naming conventions, and maintainable test code organization. Provide guidance on testing edge cases, error conditions, and performance-critical code segments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Landing Page Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for landing page management endpoints in ClinicalLog CMS. Document the GET /admin/landing-page endpoint for retrieving current landing page configuration including hero sections, about content, features display, dashboard settings, testimonials visibility, pricing plans, and navigation configuration. Detail the PUT /admin/landing-page endpoint for updating landing page content with validation rules for section visibility, image uploads, Google Drive links, and content formatting. Include request/response schemas showing JSON structure for landing page data, validation error responses, authentication requirements using Laravel Breeze middleware, and error handling patterns. Provide practical examples of content updates, bulk section toggles, and real-time preview functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Setup & Requirements
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed environment setup documentation for ClinicalLog CMS deployment. Document server requirements including PHP 8.3+, MySQL/MariaDB, and Node.js versions. Explain system prerequisites, hardware recommendations, and network requirements. Detail environment variable configuration including APP_ENV, APP_DEBUG, DB_CONNECTION, and cache settings. Document timezone configuration, locale settings, and application key generation. Include step-by-step installation procedures for different operating systems (Linux, Windows, macOS). Address permission requirements for storage and cache directories. Provide validation steps to ensure proper environment configuration before deployment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for ClinicalLog CMS. Include detailed prerequisites covering PHP 8.3+, Composer, Node.js, and database requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including repository cloning, dependency installation with Composer and npm, environment configuration, database setup, and initial migrations. Document the development server setup with Laravel Valet or local server alternatives. Include first-time setup procedures, initial user registration, and basic configuration steps. Address common installation issues and provide troubleshooting guidance. Make the content accessible to developers with varying Laravel experience levels while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System & Middleware Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the routing system and middleware architecture in ClinicalLog CMS. Document the route registration patterns, URL pattern definitions, and controller method mappings in web.php and auth.php. Explain the middleware stack including authentication, authorization, and maintenance mode protection. Detail custom middleware implementation for role-based access control and request filtering. Include examples of route parameters, named routes, and resource controllers. Document middleware ordering, conditional middleware execution, and global vs route-specific middleware application. Address route caching, performance considerations, and debugging techniques for routing issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Section Configuration & Visibility
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for section configuration and visibility controls within the content management system. Detail the boolean-based visibility toggles for each landing page section including about, features, benefits, dashboard, steps, pricing, CTA, and testimonials. Explain the section ordering system and how visibility settings affect the public-facing landing page layout. Document the configuration options for each section including title formatting, description fields, and custom styling options. Include examples of section activation workflows, visibility impact on page rendering, and best practices for section management. Address common configuration scenarios and troubleshooting visibility-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authentication flow in ClinicalLog CMS. Detail the complete login process from request initiation to session establishment. Document the LoginRequest validation class, authentication middleware, and session management. Explain the intended redirection logic, CSRF protection, and security measures. Cover error handling for failed authentication attempts, account lockout mechanisms, and security logging. Include flow diagrams showing request-response cycles, session lifecycle, and logout procedures. Address authentication customization options and integration with external authentication providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Position and Sorting System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the position-based sorting system in the Feature Management System. Detail the sort_order field implementation, automatic position calculation algorithms, and position clamping logic. Explain the complex reordering mechanisms including moving features up and down with proper position shifting. Document the database queries used for position updates, the orderBy('sort_order') pagination, and the impact of position changes on other features. Include examples of edge cases like inserting at position 1, appending to the end, and bulk position updates. Address performance considerations and database transaction handling during sorting operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Appointment Data Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Appointment data model and database schema. Detail all model attributes including name, email, whatsapp, institution, demo_date, demo_time, notes, and status fields. Document the model's relationships, accessors, mutators, and casting properties. Explain the database migration structure, field types, constraints, and indexing strategies. Include examples of querying appointments by status, date ranges, and other criteria. Address data validation rules, model events, and observers. Document data lifecycle management, soft deletes, and data retention policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for user management endpoints in ClinicalLog CMS. Document profile management APIs including GET/PUT/DELETE methods for user information updates, password changes, and account deletion. Detail authentication endpoints for login, registration, password reset, and email verification processes. Include request validation schemas, response formats, and security considerations. Provide examples of user data manipulation, role-based access control, and session management. Address user validation rules, password security requirements, and account lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Eloquent Models & Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Eloquent models in ClinicalLog CMS. Explain model structure, relationships between models, and data validation rules. Document the User model for authentication, LandingPage model with JSON casting for dynamic content, Feature model with position-based ordering, and Appointment model for contact management. Detail model attributes, accessors/mutators, scopes, and query builders. Include examples of model relationships (hasOne, belongsToMany, etc.), mass assignment protection, and model events. Explain data casting for JSON fields, attribute mutators for image handling, and model factory implementations. Provide guidance on extending models and implementing custom business logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the reusable UI component library in ClinicalLog CMS. Document each component's purpose, props/attributes, styling classes, and usage patterns. Cover form components (text-input, input-error, primary-button, secondary-button), navigation components (nav-link, responsive-nav-link), interactive elements (dropdown, modal), and layout components (application-logo, auth-session-status). Include component composition patterns, accessibility attributes, responsive design considerations, and integration with Alpine.js for interactivity. Provide practical examples showing component usage in different contexts, customization options, and best practices for extending the component library.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive feature testing documentation for ClinicalLog CMS user workflows and system interactions. Detail end-to-end testing scenarios for authentication flows, profile management, content editing, and administrative operations. Document testing patterns for user registration, login/logout processes, password management, and role-based access control. Include examples of testing landing page editing, feature management, appointment processing, and file upload operations. Cover browser automation testing, form submissions, navigation flows, and response validation. Address testing user interface interactions, session management, and state persistence across multiple requests.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Hardening & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive security hardening documentation for ClinicalLog CMS production deployment. Detail secure configuration of application keys, encryption settings, and session management. Document HTTPS enforcement, security headers configuration, and CSRF protection settings. Explain database security measures, credential management, and connection encryption. Address file upload security, image processing safety, and malicious file detection. Document authentication security including password policies, rate limiting, and two-factor authentication setup. Include firewall configuration recommendations, IP whitelisting, and access control implementation. Provide security audit procedures and vulnerability assessment guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for feature management endpoints in ClinicalLog CMS. Document all CRUD operations including GET /admin/features for listing features with sorting, POST /admin/features for creating new features with icon and position management, GET /admin/features/{id}/edit for editing individual features, PUT /admin/features/{id} for updating feature details, and DELETE /admin/features/{id} for removal. Detail the feature data model including sort_order calculations, icon management, position-based ordering, and validation rules. Include request/response schemas showing feature objects with nested properties, validation error responses, authentication requirements, and error handling patterns. Provide practical examples of feature creation, bulk sorting operations, icon uploads, and position reordering functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for ClinicalLog CMS system design. Describe the high-level MVC architecture with Laravel conventions, component interactions between controllers, models, and views. Document the routing system, middleware stack, and request-response flow. Explain the authentication architecture using Laravel Breeze, session management, and role-based access control. Include infrastructure requirements, database design patterns, and file storage architecture. Document the separation between public-facing website components and administrative interfaces. Address security considerations, data validation layers, and error handling mechanisms. Provide system context diagrams showing how different components interact and integrate with external services like Google Drive.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow & Security Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for data flow architecture and security implementation in ClinicalLog CMS. Document the complete request-response cycle from authentication to data persistence, including input validation, sanitization, and output encoding. Explain the Laravel Breeze authentication flow, session management, and CSRF protection mechanisms. Detail the request validation system using FormRequest classes, custom validation rules, and error handling strategies. Include security measures for file uploads, SQL injection prevention, and XSS protection. Document encryption implementation, secure password handling, and audit logging. Address CORS configuration, rate limiting, and security headers implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Data Models & JSON Structures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the content data models and JSON structure handling in the content management system. Explain the LandingPage model's JSON casting capabilities and how complex content structures are stored and retrieved. Document the JSON schemas for navbar links, benefits, steps, testimonials, and pricing plans including field definitions, validation rules, and data transformation processes. Detail the automatic JSON processing performed by the LandingPageController for dynamic content arrays. Include examples of data structure modifications, JSON validation patterns, and troubleshooting data integrity issues. Address performance considerations for large JSON content and optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Password Reset System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the password reset system in ClinicalLog CMS. Document the password reset workflow including link generation, email notification, token validation, and password update processes. Explain the ForgotPasswordController and NewPasswordController implementations, validation rules, and security measures. Cover email template customization, token expiration handling, and rate limiting for security. Include examples of password reset customization, multi-step verification processes, and integration with email services. Address security considerations including token generation, storage, and expiration policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Icon Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the dual icon management system in feature management. Document the integration with Lucide icons including icon_name field usage and icon display logic. Explain the file-based icon upload system with storage handling, file validation, and cleanup procedures. Detail the icon replacement workflow, delete functionality, and fallback mechanisms. Cover the storage disk configuration, file naming conventions, and public asset serving. Include examples of both icon types (Lucide vs uploaded files), validation rules for file uploads, and error handling for invalid file types or sizes. Address security considerations for file uploads and storage optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the administrative interface for managing appointment requests within the CMS dashboard. Detail the appointment listing page functionality including pagination, sorting, and filtering capabilities. Explain the status update interface, bulk operations, and appointment deletion processes. Document the user interface components, data tables, and administrative controls. Include examples of customizing the admin interface layout, adding search functionality, and implementing appointment export features. Address role-based access control for appointment management and administrative permissions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Appointment Management APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for appointment management endpoints in ClinicalLog CMS. Document the contact form submission API with POST method for appointment requests including validation, processing, and confirmation responses. Detail the administrative appointment management API covering GET for listing, PATCH for status updates, and DELETE for removal operations. Include request/response schemas for appointment data, status tracking workflows, and lead management processes. Provide examples of appointment submission, status management, and administrative operations. Address validation rules, spam prevention measures, and notification integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Factories & Seeders
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for database factories and seeders in ClinicalLog CMS. Detail the UserFactory implementation for generating test users with realistic data patterns. Explain the DatabaseSeeder class and its role in populating the database with initial data for development and testing environments. Document factory states, sequences, and faker integration for generating diverse test data. Include examples of creating related data through factory relationships, seeding different environments (development, testing, production), and customizing seed data for specific use cases. Provide guidance on extending factories for new models and implementing conditional seeding strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling with Tailwind CSS
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the styling architecture using Tailwind CSS in ClinicalLog CMS. Explain the utility-first approach, custom color palette implementation, spacing system, and responsive design breakpoints. Document the custom CSS additions in clinicallog.css for enhanced styling beyond Tailwind utilities. Detail the Tailwind configuration including custom colors, font families, spacing scales, and plugin integrations. Cover the PostCSS processing pipeline, CSS optimization for production, and asset compilation workflow. Include practical examples of component styling, responsive design patterns, dark mode considerations, and performance optimization techniques for CSS delivery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Environment & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for test environment setup and configuration in ClinicalLog CMS. Document PHPUnit configuration options, test database setup, and environment-specific settings. Explain test database management, migration handling during testing, and data seeding strategies. Include configuration for testing different environments, parallel test execution, and CI/CD integration. Cover test fixtures, mock data generation, and test cleanup procedures. Address performance optimization for test suites, memory management, and test isolation techniques. Provide guidance on debugging test failures, profiling test performance, and maintaining test reliability across development cycles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed performance optimization documentation for ClinicalLog CMS production environment. Document database optimization techniques including indexing strategies, query optimization, and connection pooling. Explain caching configuration for Redis/Memcached, file-based caching, and database query caching. Detail asset optimization including CSS/JS minification, image compression, and CDN integration. Address queue worker configuration, background job optimization, and memory management. Document browser caching strategies, HTTP/2 optimization, and load balancing setup. Include performance monitoring tools, bottleneck identification, and scaling considerations. Provide benchmarking procedures and performance testing methodologies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for ClinicalLog CMS. Detail the Laravel Breeze authentication implementation including login, registration, password reset, and email verification processes. Document the user model structure, role-based access control, and permission systems. Explain session management, middleware protection, and security measures. Include authentication flow diagrams, user registration workflows, and administrative user management. Address password policies, email verification requirements, and account security features. Provide implementation examples for customizing authentication behavior and integrating with external authentication providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interaction & Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for component interaction patterns and system integration in ClinicalLog CMS. Document how controllers communicate with models, views render data from controllers, and different subsystems interact (Landing Page, Features, Appointments). Explain the observer pattern implementation for file operations, event broadcasting for asynchronous tasks, and service container usage for dependency resolution. Detail the integration points between administrative interfaces and public-facing components. Include examples of cross-component data sharing, API communication patterns, and external service integration (Google Drive). Address component lifecycle management, memory optimization, and performance monitoring across component boundaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Image & Media Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the image and media management system within the content management interface. Detail the image upload validation including supported formats (JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP, SVG), size limitations (2MB max), and MIME type restrictions. Explain the automatic image processing workflow including storage location ('landing' directory), file naming conventions, and thumbnail generation. Document the delete functionality for replacing existing images and cleanup procedures for deleted content. Include practical examples of image upload scenarios, troubleshooting upload failures, and optimizing image performance. Address security considerations for file uploads and storage management best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Verification
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the email verification system in ClinicalLog CMS. Detail the email verification workflow including verification link generation, email dispatch, and account activation processes. Document the VerifyEmailController, EmailVerificationPromptController, and EmailVerificationNotificationController implementations. Explain verification token management, resend functionality, and verification status tracking. Cover email template customization, verification expiration handling, and user experience enhancements. Include examples of verification customization, batch verification processes, and integration with email delivery services. Address security considerations and verification bypass scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Interface Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the feature management admin interface components. Document the feature listing page with pagination, sorting controls, and bulk action capabilities. Explain the feature creation and editing forms including input validation, icon selection options, and position controls. Detail the Blade template structure, component integration, and data binding patterns. Cover the responsive design implementation, form validation feedback, and user experience enhancements. Include examples of customizing the interface, adding new form fields, and implementing additional validation rules. Address accessibility considerations and cross-browser compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Status Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the appointment status management system. Document the three status states: pending, done, and cancelled with their meanings and implications. Explain the status update workflow, validation rules, and transition logic. Detail how status changes trigger notifications, audit trails, and reporting updates. Include examples of implementing custom status states, status workflows, and automated status transitions based on time or conditions. Address status-based filtering, reporting, and analytics capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Upload & Media APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for file upload and media management endpoints in ClinicalLog CMS. Document image upload endpoints for landing page sections, feature icons, and testimonial media with supported file types, size limits, and validation rules. Detail file storage operations, URL generation, and media optimization processes. Include multipart/form-data handling, progress tracking, and error reporting mechanisms. Provide examples of file upload workflows, batch operations, and media management. Address security considerations, file validation, and storage optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: JavaScript Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for JavaScript integration and Alpine.js usage in ClinicalLog CMS. Detail the Alpine.js implementation for interactive components including mobile navigation, appointment modal, form handling, and dynamic content updates. Document the Vite build configuration, asset compilation process, and development server setup. Explain the JavaScript module organization, ES6 imports/exports, and integration with Blade templates. Cover the appointment form AJAX submission, error handling, loading states, and user feedback mechanisms. Include performance optimization techniques, bundle splitting strategies, and production deployment considerations for JavaScript assets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quality Assurance & Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive quality assurance documentation for ClinicalLog CMS testing practices. Detail code coverage analysis, test metrics interpretation, and quality gates implementation. Document testing standards, code review processes for test code, and maintainability guidelines. Include continuous integration setup, automated testing workflows, and regression testing strategies. Cover performance testing approaches, security testing methodologies, and accessibility testing procedures. Address test documentation standards, bug tracking integration, and quality reporting mechanisms. Provide guidance on test maintenance, refactoring test suites, and establishing testing culture within development teams.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive monitoring and maintenance documentation for ClinicalLog CMS production deployment. Document log rotation configuration, error tracking, and alerting mechanisms. Explain health check endpoints, uptime monitoring, and performance metrics collection. Detail backup procedures for database, files, and application code. Address automatic updates, patch management, and security update procedures. Document maintenance windows, downtime scheduling, and rollback procedures. Include database maintenance tasks, cache clearing, and storage cleanup. Provide incident response procedures, troubleshooting workflows, and escalation protocols. Address capacity planning, resource monitoring, and cost optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content management system documentation for ClinicalLog CMS. Detail the landing page management functionality including multi-section content editing, real-time preview capabilities, and section visibility controls. Document the content structure with JSON-based data models, section configuration options, and dynamic content rendering. Explain the administrative interface for content editors, WYSIWYG editing capabilities, and content validation processes. Include image management integration, Google Drive document linking, and responsive design considerations. Address content versioning, publishing workflows, and content scheduling features. Provide practical examples of content modification scenarios and troubleshooting common content management issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Interface & User Experience
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the administrative interface and user experience design of the content management system. Document the Blade template structure, component integration, and responsive design implementation. Explain the form validation feedback system, success/error messaging, and user interaction patterns. Detail the integration with Tailwind CSS for styling, Alpine.js for interactive elements, and the overall admin layout structure. Include examples of common administrative tasks, interface navigation patterns, and accessibility considerations. Address browser compatibility, mobile responsiveness, and performance optimization for the admin interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authorization Controls
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for authorization controls and access management in ClinicalLog CMS. Document role-based access control implementation, permission checking, and middleware protection. Explain password confirmation requirements, sensitive operation authorization, and profile update security measures. Cover authorization middleware usage, route protection, and view-level access control. Detail password policy enforcement, confirmation dialogs, and security prompts. Include examples of custom authorization logic, permission inheritance, and access control customization. Address security best practices, authorization auditing, and troubleshooting access issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Asset Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the asset management system in ClinicalLog CMS. Document the Vite configuration for asset compilation, development server setup, and production optimization. Explain the build pipeline including CSS preprocessing, JavaScript transpilation, asset fingerprinting, and cache busting. Detail the asset serving strategy, CDN integration possibilities, and performance optimization techniques. Cover the development workflow with hot module replacement, source maps, and debugging tools. Include production deployment considerations, asset caching strategies, and monitoring approaches for asset delivery performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed feature management system documentation for ClinicalLog CMS. Document the feature CRUD operations including creation, modification, deletion, and sorting functionality. Explain the position-based ordering system, drag-and-drop reordering capabilities, and automatic position calculation algorithms. Detail the icon management system with Lucide icons integration, custom icon uploads, and icon display logic. Include validation rules, data persistence, and frontend rendering of features. Address bulk operations, feature categorization, and search/filter functionality. Provide implementation examples for customizing feature displays and integrating new feature types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Appointment Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive appointment management system documentation for ClinicalLog CMS. Detail the contact form processing workflow, appointment data validation, and status tracking mechanisms. Document the appointment model structure, including fields for contact information, appointment preferences, and status management. Explain the administrative interface for viewing, responding to, and managing appointment requests. Include email notification systems, automated response generation, and appointment scheduling integration possibilities. Address data privacy considerations, appointment analytics, and export capabilities. Provide examples of customizing appointment workflows and integrating with external calendar systems.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation for ClinicalLog CMS. Detail all database tables, their relationships, and field definitions. Address database performance considerations, query optimization strategies, and data integrity measures. Document the factory and seeder implementations for testing and development. Provide examples of customizing models and extending the database schema.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for ClinicalLog CMS. Detail the Blade template system, component library structure, and layout inheritance patterns. Explain the JavaScript integration with Alpine.js, asset compilation through Vite, and build process optimization. Include the component library with reusable UI elements, form handling patterns, and interactive features. Address accessibility compliance, cross-browser compatibility, and performance optimization techniques. Document the asset pipeline, caching strategies, and production deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for ClinicalLog CMS RESTful endpoints. Document all HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Include endpoint categories for content management, user administration, file operations, and appointment processing. Detail request parameter validation, response formatting, and error handling patterns. Explain authentication middleware, rate limiting, and security considerations for each endpoint. Include practical examples of API usage, common request patterns, and integration guidelines. Address CORS configuration, API versioning strategies, and backward compatibility considerations. Provide client implementation examples and troubleshooting guidance for common API integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation for ClinicalLog CMS. Document unit testing approaches for models and business logic, feature testing for user workflows, and integration testing for system components. Explain test environment setup, database testing strategies, and mocking techniques. Include testing best practices, continuous integration considerations, and test coverage analysis. Address performance testing, security testing, and regression testing procedures. Provide examples of writing effective tests, test data management, and automated testing workflows.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and configuration documentation for ClinicalLog CMS. Detail production environment setup, server requirements, and security hardening procedures. Document CI/CD pipeline setup, automated deployment processes, and environment-specific configurations. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common deployment issues and maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# ClinicalLog CMS - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
ClinicalLog CMS is a Laravel-based Content Management System designed specifically for medical education institutions. The platform serves as both a marketing website and administrative dashboard for managing clinical logbook systems used by medical students, lecturers, and healthcare institutions.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Primary Goal**: Provide an integrated solution for medical institutions to showcase their clinical logbook capabilities while maintaining administrative control over content
- **Content Management**: Enable non-technical staff to manage landing page content, features, testimonials, and institutional data
- **User Experience**: Deliver a modern, responsive interface with real-time content updates and appointment management
- **Scalability**: Support growing medical institutions with customizable sections and multi-institutional data handling

### Target Audience
- Medical schools and universities seeking digital transformation
- Healthcare institutions implementing clinical competency tracking
- Educational technology companies serving the medical sector
- Administrators managing medical training programs

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The project follows Laravel's MVC architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend Interface] --> B[Laravel Controllers]
    B --> C[Eloquent Models]
    C --> D[Database Layer]
    
    E[Admin Dashboard] --> F[Feature Management]
    E --> G[Landing Page CMS]
    E --> H[Appointment System]
    E --> I[User Administration]
    
    J[Public Website] --> K[Static Content]
    J --> L[Dynamic Data]
    J --> M[Contact Forms]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Repository Pattern**: Used for data access through Eloquent models
- **Command Pattern**: Implemented for complex operations like feature reordering
- **Observer Pattern**: Applied for file upload and deletion operations
- **Template Method**: Utilized in controller actions for consistent CRUD operations

### System Relationships
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant C as Controller
    participant M as Model
    participant DB as Database
    
    U->>C: HTTP Request
    C->>M: Validate & Process Data
    M->>DB: Execute Query
    DB-->>M: Return Results
    M-->>C: Processed Data
    C-->>U: Rendered Response
```

### Data Flow Diagrams
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[Form Validation]
    B --> C[Controller Processing]
    C --> D[Model Operations]
    D --> E[Database Storage]
    E --> F[Response Generation]
    F --> G[View Rendering]
    G --> H[User Feedback]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Web Routes**: `/routes/web.php` - Primary application routing with authentication middleware
- **Composer Configuration**: `/composer.json` - Dependency management and autoloading setup
- **Package Configuration**: `/package.json` - Frontend asset pipeline configuration

### Core Modules
- **Landing Page Management**: `/app/Http/Controllers/LandingPageController.php` - Comprehensive CMS for homepage content
- **Feature Management**: `/app/Http/Controllers/FeatureController.php` - Dynamic feature listing with drag-and-drop reordering
- **Appointment System**: `/app/Http/Controllers/AppointmentController.php` - Contact form processing with status management
- **Authentication**: `/app/Http/Controllers/Auth/` - Complete Laravel Breeze authentication suite

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Configuration**: `/config/app.php` - Application-wide settings and environment variables
- **Frontend Build**: `/vite.config.js` - Asset compilation and development server configuration
- **Styling Framework**: `/tailwind.config.js` - Tailwind CSS customization for consistent design system

### External Dependencies
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Laravel Framework] --> B[PHP 8.3+]
    A --> C[Blade Templates]
    A --> D[Eloquent ORM]
    
    E[Frontend Tools] --> F[Tailwind CSS]
    E --> G[Vite Build Tool]
    E --> H[Alpine.js]
    
    I[Development Tools] --> J[PHPUnit Testing]
    I --> K[PestPHP]
    I --> L[Mockery]
```

### Integration Points
- **Storage System**: Integrated with Laravel's filesystem abstraction for image uploads
- **Authentication**: Built on Laravel Breeze with email verification and password reset
- **Asset Pipeline**: Vite-powered compilation with hot module replacement for development
- **Database Abstraction**: MySQL/MariaDB support with comprehensive migration system

### Component Relationship Diagrams
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Admin Layout] --> B[Navigation Sidebar]
    A --> C[Main Content Area]
    
    B --> D[Dashboard Link]
    B --> E[Features Management]
    B --> F[Landing Page Editor]
    B --> G[Appointments]
    B --> H[User Management]
    
    C --> I[CRUD Operations]
    C --> J[Form Handling]
    C --> K[File Uploads]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The system provides comprehensive content management capabilities tailored for medical education institutions:

#### Landing Page CMS
- Multi-section content management (Hero, About, Features, Benefits, Dashboard)
- Real-time preview functionality
- Image upload with automatic optimization
- JSON-based content structures for flexible data handling

#### Feature Management System
- Drag-and-drop reordering with automatic position adjustment
- Dual icon system supporting both custom uploads and Lucide icons
- Sort order preservation during CRUD operations
- Responsive grid layout for feature presentation

#### Appointment Management
- Contact form with validation and sanitization
- Status tracking (Pending, Approved, Completed)
- Institutional data collection for demonstration requests
- Administrative dashboard for lead management

#### User Administration
- Role-based access control (Administrator)
- Profile management with password security
- Email verification workflow
- Session management and security headers

### Implementation Highlights
- **Image Management**: `/app/Http/Controllers/LandingPageController.php` - Sophisticated file upload handling with delete functionality
- **Dynamic Content**: `/resources/views/admin/landingpage.blade.php` - Tabbed interface with real-time content editing
- **Data Persistence**: `/database/migrations/` - Comprehensive schema design supporting complex content structures
- **Responsive Design**: `/resources/views/layouts/admin.blade.php` - Mobile-first admin interface with sidebar navigation

### Feature Architecture Diagrams
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ContentEditing
    ContentEditing --> PreviewMode: Switch Tab
    PreviewMode --> ContentEditing: Edit Content
    ContentEditing --> ImageUpload: Upload Media
    ImageUpload --> ContentEditing: Upload Complete
    ContentEditing --> SaveChanges: Submit Form
    SaveChanges --> [*]: Success Message
    SaveChanges --> ContentEditing: Validation Error
```

### Data Management Flow
```mermaid
erDiagram
    LANDING_PAGE ||--o{ FEATURES : contains
    LANDING_PAGE ||--o{ TESTIMONIALS : displays
    LANDING_PAGE ||--o{ NAVIGATION_LINKS : includes
    
    FEATURES ||--o{ ICONS : uses
    APPOINTMENTS ||--|| USERS : requested_by
    USERS ||--o{ APPOINTMENTS : created
    
    STORAGE ||--o{ LANDING_IMAGES : stores
    STORAGE ||--o{ FEATURE_ICONS : stores
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
ClinicalLog CMS demonstrates a well-structured Laravel application with clear separation between frontend presentation and backend administration. The modular design allows for easy maintenance and future feature expansion while maintaining consistency across the medical education domain.

### Architectural Strengths
- **Clean MVC Implementation**: Proper separation of concerns with dedicated controllers, models, and views
- **Comprehensive Authentication**: Full Laravel Breeze integration with email verification and password reset
- **Flexible Content Management**: JSON-based content structures allowing for dynamic content without schema changes
- **Modern Development Workflow**: Vite-powered asset pipeline with hot reloading for efficient development

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **API Layer**: Consider implementing RESTful API endpoints for mobile app integration
- **Caching Strategy**: Add Redis caching for frequently accessed landing page content
- **Testing Coverage**: Expand unit and feature tests beyond current authentication suite
- **Performance Optimization**: Implement database indexing for large datasets and optimize image delivery

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement API Endpoints**: Create dedicated API routes for mobile applications and third-party integrations
2. **Add Analytics Integration**: Integrate Google Analytics or similar tools for usage tracking
3. **Enhance Security**: Implement rate limiting for contact forms and additional CSRF protection
4. **Performance Monitoring**: Add database query logging and performance metrics collection
5. **Documentation**: Create comprehensive API documentation and developer guides

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Mobile Application**: Develop companion mobile app for offline feature viewing
- **Multi-Institution Support**: Extend user management to support multiple institutional accounts
- **Advanced Analytics**: Implement detailed reporting for educational outcomes and competency tracking
- **Integration APIs**: Create webhook system for external learning management systems

This project represents a solid foundation for medical education technology platforms, with clear pathways for scaling and enhancement based on institutional needs.

---

**Sources:**
- [composer.json](composer.json)
- [package.json](package.json)
- [web.php](routes/web.php)
- [LandingPageController.php](app/Http/Controllers/LandingPageController.php)
- [FeatureController.php](app/Http/Controllers/FeatureController.php)
- [LandingPage.php](app/Models/LandingPage.php)
- [Feature.php](app/Models/Feature.php)
- [Appointment.php](app/Models/Appointment.php)
- [landingpage.blade.php](resources/views/admin/landingpage.blade.php)
- [admin.blade.php](resources/views/layouts/admin.blade.php)
- [landing.blade.php](resources/views/landing.blade.php)
- [tailwind.config.js](tailwind.config.js)
- [vite.config.js](vite.config.js)
- [app.php](config/app.php)
- [2026_06_22_024652_create_appointments_table.php](database/migrations/2026_06_22_024652_create_appointments_table.php)

</blog>
